senses.definition: through the force, effort, or influence of someone or something

examples.x: She achieved her success by dint of hard work and determination.
The company became a market leader by dint of its innovative products.
He rose to prominence by dint of his hard work and dedication.
By dint of his charisma, he was able to sway the opinions of others.
They overcame the challenges by dint of their teamwork and determination.

senses.definition: by using or with the help of something

examples.x: He communicated with his family by means of letters and phone calls.
He created a masterpiece by means of his imagination and talent.
She cured her insomnia by means of meditation and yoga.

senses.definition: through a particular method, route, or means

examples.x: We will travel to the conference by way of the express train for a faster journey.
She sent the package to her friend by way of express mail.
They communicated with each other by way of email.
The goods were transported to the warehouse by way of truck.
He obtained the information by way of an online search.

senses.definition: because of a particular quality, attribute, or right possessed by someone or something

examples.x: She became the leader by virtue of her experience and expertise in the field.
He was granted access to the meeting in virtue of his position as the team leader.
She was granted access to the exclusive club by virtue of her membership.
The organization was able to secure funding in virtue of its reputation for excellence.
He was appointed as the team captain by virtue of his leadership skills.

senses.definition: utilizing a particular method, tool, or technique

examples.x: The problem was solved through the use of advanced computer algorithms.
The company improved its efficiency through the use of new technology.
She achieved her goals through the use of effective time management techniques.
The students learned complex concepts through the use of interactive multimedia presentations.
The artist created stunning visual effects through the use of digital editing software.

senses.definition: used to convey that something is happening or exists after and often due to another event or action

examples.x: In the wake of the hurricane, the community rallied together to support those affected.
New safety regulations were implemented in the wake of the recent workplace accident.
In the wake of the economic downturn, many businesses had to downsize or close.

senses.definition: used to introduce the reason of something happening

examples.x: He missed the bus because of the traffic jam.
They canceled the party because of the pandemic.
He quit his job because of his boss.
She loves him because of his kindness.

senses.definition: because of a particular cause or reason

examples.x: The event was canceled by reason of inclement weather.
She was unable to attend the meeting by reason of a prior commitment.
He was granted an extension on the deadline by reason of illness.
The policy was changed by reason of feedback from customers.
The decision was made by reason of safety concerns.

senses.definition: as a result of a specific cause or reason

examples.x: The flight delay was due to heavy fog at the airport.
The event was canceled due to unforeseen circumstances.
He missed the meeting due to a family emergency.
The increase in prices was due to inflation.
The cancellation of classes was due to a teacher strike.

senses.definition: because of a specific reason or cause

examples.x: The event was postponed on account of the unexpected rainstorm.
She was promoted on account of her outstanding performance.
He couldn't attend the meeting on account of illness.
The project was delayed on account of funding issues.
They decided to relocate on account of better job opportunities.

senses.definition: as a result of a particular cause or circumstance

examples.x: Owing to his hard work and dedication, he received a promotion at work.
The match was canceled owing to heavy rain.
He missed the deadline owing to a family emergency.
They were able to finish early owing to efficient teamwork.
She was unable to attend owing to prior commitments.

senses.definition: as a result of actions or treatment carried out by a particular person or group

examples.x: The prisoner suffered abuse at the hands of the prison guards.
The village was destroyed at the hands of invading forces.
Many innocent civilians lost their lives at the hands of the dictator.
The patient received inadequate care at the hands of negligent doctors.
The company went bankrupt at the hands of incompetent management.

senses.definition: used to express the cause or reason for a particular outcome

examples.x: Thanks to the timely intervention of the firefighters, the house was saved from complete destruction by the fire.
The event was a success thanks to the hard work of the organizing committee.
They were able to finish the project ahead of schedule thanks to efficient teamwork.
The students performed well on the exam thanks to the thorough preparation provided by their teacher.
The problem was resolved quickly thanks to the expertise of the technician.

senses.definition: used to indicate the necessary condition or requirement that must be fulfilled for a particular goal or outcome to be achieved

examples.x: In order for the project to succeed, all team members must collaborate effectively.
She had to study diligently in order for her to pass the exam.
In order for the team to win the game, they need to work together effectively.
He must complete his training in order for him to qualify for the promotion.
In order for the plant to thrive, it requires regular watering and sunlight.

senses.definition: having control or responsibility for someone or something

examples.x: The manager is in charge of overseeing daily operations.
He is in charge of overseeing the construction project.
She is in charge of the marketing department.
The principal is in charge of the school's daily operations.
The director is in charge of casting actors for the upcoming film.
